prevalence.source: European child development epidemiology syntheses / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: increasing due to expanded diagnostic criteria and improved detection

incidence.value: 3-6 per 1,000 live births (diagnosed over early childhood)

vulnerablePopulations: children with limited access to specialist assessment in underserved regions
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 criteria with multidisciplinary assessment; standardized tools commonly used in specialist services

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; developmental screening and school pathways differ by member state
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Not applicable; focus is functional support and inclusion

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Structured referral pathways through pediatric and educational services in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Early intervention, speech/occupational therapy, educational accommodations, caregiver support; medication for comorbidities when indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Service demand and wait times for diagnostic assessment in some member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established multidisciplinary services and school-based supports in many health systems
